page_content:
Formerly: HONR218R.

PLSC452 Environmental Horticulture (3 Credits)

Environmental horticulture principles used in the establishment and maintenance of plant materials in residential and commercial landscapes will be addressed. The effect of soil conditions, environmental factors, and commercial practices will be discussed in relation to the growth

and development of newly-installed plant materials. Field diagnostics

will be used by students to assess significant problems of plant decline. Environmental sustainability will be combined with current commercial practices of storm water management, nutrient management, and irrigation management to achieve an integrated approach to plant management.

Prerequisite: Minimum grade of C- in PLSC110 and PLSC111; or minimum grade of C- in PLSC112 and PLSC113; and minimum grade of C- in PLSC253 and PLSC254.

PLSC453 Weed Science (3 Credits) Weed identification, ecology, and control (cultural, mechanical, biological, and chemical methods).
